Since FS!QR automatically deletes files after a set period, make a habit of removing any data you no longer need promptly, and handle IDs and passwords with care. Keep your devices protected with up-to-date antivirus software, and maintain strong security practices day-to-day to prevent unauthorized access.
さらに、組織内では万一のインシデント発生時に備えた連絡体制を整備し、被害の拡大を: Organizations should also have a clear incident response chain of communication in place before anything goes wrong, along with predefined procedures to contain the damage. Participating in regular security drills and staying current on the latest attack techniques helps individual users recognize threats before they escalate.
オンラインでファイルを共有するときには、利便性の裏に潜むリスクを見逃してはなりま: When sharing files online, it's easy to overlook the risks that come with the convenience. If a link ends up in the wrong hands, it can lead to unintended data exposure or unauthorized access. Without a password in place, files can even surface in search engine results or be accessed from shared devices.

リスクと対策の考え方 | FS!QRファイル共有セキュリティ対策: Understanding Risks and Countermeasures | FS!QR File Sharing Security Guide
リスクを完全にゼロにすることはできませんが、脅威を理解し対策を継続的に実施するこ: Risk can never be reduced to zero, but understanding threats and consistently applying countermeasures can dramatically lower the chances of harm. Reviewing your sharing tool settings and committing to proper user education form the foundation of secure, access-controlled file sharing. Small daily habits add up to meaningful protection for your entire organization.
リスク管理では、危険をゼロにすることよりも、起こりやすい事故を把握し、被害が広が: In risk management, it is more realistic to understand likely incidents and prepare to stop damage before it spreads than to try to eliminate every danger. For file sharing, clarifying who handles what, for how long, and on which device makes the priority countermeasures easier to see.
不審なアクセス、誤送信、想定外の再共有に気付けるよう、利用者へ報告先を周知します: Tell users where to report suspicious access, mistaken sending, or unexpected resharing.
不要になったリンクは削除または無効化する: Delete or disable links that are no longer needed.
人の心理につけ込むソーシャルエンジニアリングも警戒が必要です。信頼できる人物を装: Social engineering — which exploits human psychology rather than technical vulnerabilities — is another threat to watch out for. When a link arrives in an email or message that appears to come from someone trusted, users instinctively click. Incidents involving malware planted through such links or stolen credentials are on the rise.
人を狙う攻撃にも備える: Prepare for attacks that target people
共有リンクをSNSや公開チャットに貼らない: Do not post shared links on social media or public chats.
共有前にファイル内容、宛先、保存期間、パスワードの有無を確認します。: Before sharing, confirm the file contents, recipient, storage period, and whether a password is set.
問題が起きたら共有を止め、関係者へ連絡し、必要に応じてパスワード変更や再発防止策: If a problem occurs, stop sharing, contact the relevant people, and change passwords or apply recurrence prevention measures as needed.
普段やり取りしている相手からの連絡でも、文面が不自然、添付ファイル名が曖昧、UR: Even if a message appears to come from someone you usually contact, confirm through another channel if the wording feels unnatural, the attachment name is vague, or the URL domain differs from usual. A few seconds of confirmation can prevent data leakage or device infection.
通信経路とリンク漏えいのリスク: Risks in communication paths and leaked links
通信経路の盗聴も大きな脅威です。暗号化されていないHTTPを利用してファイルを送: Eavesdropping on the communication channel is another serious threat. Sending or receiving files over unencrypted HTTP leaves data vulnerable to tampering or interception along the way. Man-in-the-middle attacks can go even further, silently swapping files in transit without the user ever noticing.
ファイル共有のリスクを場面別に切り分ける方法: How to Break Down File-Sharing Risks by Situation
ファイル共有の事故を、宛先違い・リンク漏えい・端末・保存期間の四つに分けて考える: This practical guide divides file-sharing incidents into four areas: wrong recipient, leaked link, device, and retention period. It explains with concrete examples what FS!QR can address and what the service cannot solve.

ファイル共有 リスク, 誤送信, リンク漏えい, フィッシング, インシデント対: file-sharing risks, misdirected sends, leaked links, phishing, incident response, FS QR
ファイル共有の安全性を「暗号化されているか」だけで判断すると、事故の原因を見落と: Judging file-sharing safety only by whether it is encrypted can hide the cause of an incident. Dividing common problems into recipient, link, device, and end of sharing makes it easier to decide which safeguards are needed. This guide explains the checking order for temporary-sharing services such as FS!QR, together with their limits.
最も確実な対策は、アップロード前にファイルを開いて宛先と内容を確認することです。: The most reliable safeguard is to open the file and check the recipient and contents before uploading. If autocomplete selected a similarly named contact, compare the person's name, company, and request before sending the URL. When sending several materials together, remove unnecessary personal information and internal notes and keep only the files needed for the purpose.
送信後に宛先違いへ気付いた場合は、相手へ削除を依頼する前に、まず共有ページを停止: If you notice the wrong recipient after sending, first stop or delete the sharing page before asking that person to delete it. The service cannot remove a copy or screenshot they have already saved. Promptly report the possible misdirected send and the affected data to the responsible person in your organization.
リンク漏えい：URLを秘密情報として扱う: Leaked Link: Treat the URL as Confidential Information
共有URLは、パスワードが別に設定されていても、公開掲示板や大人数のチャットへ貼: Even when a separate password is set, it is safer not to post the sharing URL on public boards or in large chats. Anyone who sees it can try to access it, so use a channel limited to the intended recipients. When displaying a QR code in a meeting, also check where outsiders could photograph it, recordings, and the scope of screen sharing.
リンクを受け取った側は、送信者から予告された共有か、ドメインが正しいか、パスワー: The recipient should check whether the share was announced by the sender, whether the domain is correct, and whether the password screen looks legitimate. The more a message pressures you with phrases such as “check urgently” or “required today,” the more important it is to confirm with the person through another channel.
端末の問題：受信後のコピーを管理する: Device Issues: Manage Copies After Receipt
HTTPSやファイルの暗号化は、端末のウイルス感染、画面の覗き見、誤った保存先、: HTTPS and file encryption do not prevent a device infection, shoulder surfing, an incorrect save location, or redistribution by the recipient. On a shared PC, a file may remain in the Downloads folder or browser history. Decide where to move it and when to delete it after receipt, and handle personal and business devices differently.
期限管理：終わりを決めてから共有する: Expiry Management: Set the End Before Sharing
短期間の受け渡しなら、作業に必要な期間だけ保存期限を設定します。期限が切れるとサ: For a short-term handoff, set a retention period only as long as the work requires. After expiry, the shared data managed by the service is no longer available, but files saved on the recipient's device are not deleted. Shortening the period reduces how long data remains in the storage location; it does not control the recipient's actions.
問題が起きたときの三段階: Three Stages When Something Goes Wrong
共有を削除または停止し、URLとパスワードの再利用を止める。: Delete or stop the share and prevent reuse of the URL and password.
対象ファイル、宛先、時刻、保存された可能性を責任者へ伝える。: Tell the responsible person the affected file, recipient, time, and possibility that it was saved.
止める：共有を削除または停止し、URLとパスワードの再利用を止める。: Stop: delete or stop the share and prevent reuse of the URL and password.
知らせる：対象ファイル、宛先、時刻、保存された可能性を責任者へ伝える。: Notify: tell the responsible person the affected file, recipient, time, and possibility that it was saved.
振り返る：宛先確認、版管理、連絡手段、期限設定のどこを変えるか決める。: Review: decide what to change in recipient checks, version control, communication channels, or expiry settings.

監査記録や厳格な本人確認が必要な情報は、最初から承認済みの文書管理システムを使っ: For information requiring audit records or strict identity verification, use an approved document-management system from the start. FS!QR shortens the procedure for a temporary handoff; it does not replace your organization's incident response or retention rules.
